instruction:
UsernameValidator allows trailing newline in usernames
Description
	
ASCIIUsernameValidator and UnicodeUsernameValidator use the regex 
r'^[\w.@+-]+$'
The intent is to only allow alphanumeric characters as well as ., @, +, and -. However, a little known quirk of Python regexes is that $ will also match a trailing newline. Therefore, the user name validators will accept usernames which end with a newline. You can avoid this behavior by instead using \A and \Z to terminate regexes. For example, th
output:
<<PATCH
diff --git a/django/contrib/auth/validators.py b/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
index b4878cfd45..1304f20a60 100644
--- a/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
+++ b/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _
 
 @deconstructible
 c

--- a/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
+++ b/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _
 
 @deconstructible
 c

instruction:
Autoreloader with StatReloader doesn't track changes in manage.py.
Description
	 
		(last modified by Mariusz Felisiak)
	 
This is a bit convoluted, but here we go.
Environment (OSX 10.11):
$ python -V
Python 3.6.2
$ pip -V
pip 19.1.1
$ pip install Django==2.2.1
Steps to reproduce:
Run a server python manage.py runserver
Edit the manage.py file, e.g. add print(): 
def main():
	print('sth')
	os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'ticket_30479.settings')
	...
Under 2.1.8 (and prior), thi
output: Now I understand the issue. Let me look at how the `__main__` module's `__spec__` behaves when running `python manage.py runserver`:Now I understand the issue. When running `python manage.py runserver`, the `manage.py` script becomes the `__main__` module. The `__main__` module's `__spec__` is typic

instruction:
UsernameValidator allows trailing newline in usernames
Description
	
ASCIIUsernameValidator and UnicodeUsernameValidator use the regex 
r'^[\w.@+-]+$'
The intent is to only allow alphanumeric characters as well as ., @, +, and -. However, a little known quirk of Python regexes is that $ will also match a trailing newline. Therefore, the user name validators will accept usernames which end with a newline. You can avoid this behavior by instead using \A and \Z to terminate regexes. For example, th
output:
Let me verify the change:<<PATCH
diff --git a/django/contrib/auth/validators.py b/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
index b84f75b..b1e95e5 100644
--- a/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
+++ b/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

instruction:
Add get_response_async for ASGIStaticFilesHandler
Description
	
It looks like the StaticFilesHandlerMixin is missing the the async response function.
Without this, when trying to use the ASGIStaticFilesHandler, this is the traceback:
Exception inside application: 'NoneType' object is not callable
Traceback (most recent call last):
 File ".../lib/python3.7/site-packages/daphne/cli.py", line 30, in asgi
	await self.app(scope, receive, send)
 File ".../src/django/django/contrib/staticfiles/handlers
output: Now I understand the issue. The `StaticFilesHandlerMixin` has `get_response` but not `get_response_async`. When `ASGIHandler.__call__` runs, it calls `self.get_response_async(request)`, which comes from `BaseHandler`. But the `load_middleware` is overridden to do nothing (so `_middleware_chain` is n

instruction:
django-admin startapp with trailing slash in directory name results in error
Description
	
Bash tab-completion appends trailing slashes to directory names. django-admin startapp name directory/ results in the error:
CommandError: '' is not a valid app directory. Please make sure the directory is a valid identifier.
The error is caused by ​line 77 of django/core/management/templates.py by calling basename() on the path with no consideration for a trailing slash:
self.validate_name(os.path.basenam
output:
<<PATCH
diff --git a/django/core/management/templates.py b/django/core/management/templates.py
index 6a49a16f5b..6d6e0c5e1c 100644
--- a/django/core/management/templates.py
+++ b/django/core/management/templates.py
@@ -74,7 +74,7 @@ class TemplateCommand(BaseCommand):
                 raise CommandE

instruction:
Changing user's email could invalidate password reset tokens
Description
	
Sequence:
Have account with email address foo@…
Password reset request for that email (unused)
foo@… account changes their email address
Password reset email is used
The password reset email's token should be rejected at that point, but in fact it is allowed.
The fix is to add the user's email address into ​PasswordResetTokenGenerator._make_hash_value()
Nothing forces a user to even have an email as per AbstractBaseUser.
output: Now let me verify the fix looks correct:Let me run the existing tests to make sure nothing is broken:All 7 existing token tests pass. Now let me verify the fix handles the edge case properly by checking the test file to understand the test patterns:The fix looks good. All tests pass. The change is m

instruction:
dbshell additional parameters should be passed before dbname on PostgreSQL.
Description
	
psql expects all options to proceed the database name, if provided. So, if doing something like `./manage.py dbshell -- -c "select * from some_table;" one will get this:
$ ./manage.py dbshell -- -c "select * from some_table;"
psql: warning: extra command-line argument "-c" ignored
psql: warning: extra command-line argument "select * from some_table;" ignored
psql (10.21)
Type "help" for help.
some_database=
output:
The issue is clear. On line 36-37, the dbname is added before the parameters. For `psql`, the database name must come after all options. Let me fix this:Let me verify the fix:<<PATCH
diff --git a/django/db/backends/postgresql/client.py b/django/db/backends/postgresql/client.py
index cf3c169de5..7f0d

instruction:
Add get_response_async for ASGIStaticFilesHandler
Description
	
It looks like the StaticFilesHandlerMixin is missing the the async response function.
Without this, when trying to use the ASGIStaticFilesHandler, this is the traceback:
Exception inside application: 'NoneType' object is not callable
Traceback (most recent call last):
 File ".../lib/python3.7/site-packages/daphne/cli.py", line 30, in asgi
	await self.app(scope, receive, send)
 File ".../src/django/django/contrib/staticfiles/handlers
output: Now I can see the issue. `StaticFilesHandlerMixin` has `get_response` but no `get_response_async`. When `ASGIStaticFilesHandler.__call__` invokes `super().__call__()` which is `ASGIHandler.__call__`, it calls `self.get_response_async(request)`. Since there's no `get_response_async` on the mixin, it

instruction:
UsernameValidator allows trailing newline in usernames
Description
	
ASCIIUsernameValidator and UnicodeUsernameValidator use the regex 
r'^[\w.@+-]+$'
The intent is to only allow alphanumeric characters as well as ., @, +, and -. However, a little known quirk of Python regexes is that $ will also match a trailing newline. Therefore, the user name validators will accept usernames which end with a newline. You can avoid this behavior by instead using \A and \Z to terminate regexes. For example, th
output:
Let me verify the fix works:The newline is now properly rejected (raises `ValidationError`). Let me verify normal usernames still pass:Normal usernames pass (returns `None` = no error), and usernames with trailing newline are rejected (raises `ValidationError`). The fix works correctly.

<<PATCH
dif

Description

self.validate_name(os.path.basenam

instruction:
UsernameValidator allows trailing newline in usernames
Description
	
ASCIIUsernameValidator and UnicodeUsernameValidator use the regex 
r'^[\w.@+-]+$'
The intent is to only allow alphanumeric characters as well as ., @, +, and -. However, a little known quirk of Python regexes is that $ will also match a trailing newline. Therefore, the user name validators will accept usernames which end with a newline. You can avoid this behavior by instead using \A and \Z to terminate regexes. For example, th

--- a/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
+++ b/django/contrib/auth/validators.py
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _

instruction:
Changing user's email could invalidate password reset tokens
Description
	
Sequence:
Have account with email address foo@…
Password reset request for that email (unused)
foo@… account changes their email address
Password reset email is used
The password reset email's token should be rejected at that point, but in fact it is allowed.
The fix is to add the user's email address into ​PasswordResetTokenGenerator._make_hash_value()
Nothing forces a user to even have an email as per AbstractBaseUser.

Now let me verify the fix handles the edge case properly by checking the test file to understand the test patterns:The fix looks good. All tests pass. The change is m

instruction:
dbshell additional parameters should be passed before dbname on PostgreSQL.
Description
	
psql expects all options to proceed the database name, if provided. So, if doing something like `./manage.py dbshell -- -c "select * from some_table;" one will get this:
$ ./manage.py dbshell -- -c "select * from some_table;"
psql: warning: extra command-line argument "-c" ignored
psql: warning: extra command-line argument "select * from some_table;" ignored
psql (10.21)
Type "help" for help.
some_database=
output:
The issue is clear. On line 36-37, the dbname is added before the parameters. For `psql`, the database name must come after all options. Let me fix this:Let me verify the fix:<<PATCH
diff --git a/django/db/backends/postgresql/client.py b/django/db/backends/postgresql/client.py
index cf3c169de5..7f0d

instruction:
Add get_response_async for ASGIStaticFilesHandler
Description
	
It looks like the StaticFilesHandlerMixin is missing the the async response function.
Without this, when trying to use the ASGIStaticFilesHandler, this is the traceback:
Exception inside application: 'NoneType' object is not callable
Traceback (most recent call last):
 File ".../lib/python3.7/site-packages/daphne/cli.py", line 30, in asgi
	await self.app(scope, receive, send)
 File ".../src/django/django/contrib/staticfiles/handlers
output: Now I can see the issue. `StaticFilesHandlerMixin` has `get_response` but no `get_response_async`. When `ASGIStaticFilesHandler.__call__` invokes `super().__call__()` which is `ASGIHandler.__call__`, it calls `self.get_response_async(request)`. Since there's no `get_response_async` on the mixin, it

instruction:
Autoreloader with StatReloader doesn't track changes in manage.py.
Description
	 
		(last modified by Mariusz Felisiak)
	 
This is a bit convoluted, but here we go.
Environment (OSX 10.11):
$ python -V
Python 3.6.2
$ pip -V
pip 19.1.1
$ pip install Django==2.2.1
Steps to reproduce:
Run a server python manage.py runserver
Edit the manage.py file, e.g. add print(): 
def main():
	print('sth')
	os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'ticket_30479.settings')
	...
Under 2.1.8 (and prior), thi
